description: Combat AI configuration — attack parameters, flanking logic, charge paths, trigger zones

combat_triggers.label: CombatTrigger_TRex_001
combat_triggers.notes: Player entering this box triggers T-Rex charge sequence

combat_triggers.label: CombatTrigger_Raptor_001
combat_triggers.notes: Player entering triggers raptor pack split into flanking formation

combat_triggers.label: CombatTrigger_WaterSource_001
combat_triggers.notes: Highest tension zone — T-Rex and raptors both converge here. Player caught between species.

Brachiosaurus.notes: Normally passive — only attacks if player gets too close or throws objects

RaptorAmbush_Zone.coordination_logic: Front raptor engages first. Left+right wait 2s then attack simultaneously from sides. If player turns to face a flanker, front raptor attacks from behind.

trex_charge_path.charge_logic: T-Rex locks on player position at start of charge. Follows straight line through markers. Cannot course-correct mid-charge. Player survival strategy: dodge perpendicular to charge vector.

combat_design_principles.fairness: Every attack has a readable tell animation (0.4-0.8s window). Player always has a survival option if they read the tell.
combat_design_principles.tension: Player never knows if they will win until the last second. Outcome determined by player skill, not RNG.
combat_design_principles.raptor_philosophy: Raptors are terrifying because they coordinate. One raptor alone is manageable. Three is a death sentence unless player uses terrain.
combat_design_principles.trex_philosophy: T-Rex is unstoppable in open ground. Survival requires using trees, rocks, narrow gaps the T-Rex cannot fit through.
combat_design_principles.water_source_design: Water source is the highest-tension area because both predator species converge there. Player must choose: risk dehydration or risk predator encounter.
